key_items: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash with RSI suggesting potential 30-50% rebounds to $70K
Bitcoin bears face $2.6B short trap as funding rates drop - potential short squeeze setup
BlackRock dumps $213M in Bitcoin as institutional selling pressure continues amid macro headwinds
reasoning: Bitcoin is deeply oversold with technical indicators (RSI at 2020 levels) suggesting mean reversion upside is possible in a 1-7 day swing, but institutional selling pressure (BlackRock liquidation), negative macro backdrop (stronger-than-expected jobs data pressuring rates), and a 50%+ crash from Trump peaks create conflicting signals favoring caution. The $2.6B short positioning could trigger relief rallies but lacks conviction given regulatory uncertainty (CLARITY Act momentum slowing), capital deployment competition from AI spending boom, and weakening retail participation.
risks: Further institutional capitulation and breakdown below $60K support could cascade into $50-55K as the 2020 crash analogy breaks down and macro conditions diverge from previous bull cycle patterns.
